feat: offline file uploads with image/video compression (Session 4)

Photos compressed via Canvas API (5MB→300KB), videos capped at 1Mbps
bitrate (15MB→6MB). Files queue in IDB when offline, sync via FormData
through Axios on reconnect.

- compress-image.ts: Canvas API compression (max 1920px, JPEG 80%)

- cae-tab.tsx: handleFileUpload uses offlineCaeService (online + offline)

- mutation-queue.ts: IDB CRUD with 5-retry limit

- layout.tsx: SyncStatus component + online event listener + periodic sync

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

Extract resolveProjectDir to deduplicate session path logic (#725)

## Summary
- Extracts ~20 lines of duplicated project directory resolution code
from `listSessions` and `readSessionTranscript` into a private
`resolveProjectDir` method
- Adds 7 new tests covering the shared resolution logic through both
public methods

Fixes #538

## Changes
- **`src/main/orchestrators/claude-code-provider.ts`**: Added private
`resolveProjectDir(cwd, profileEnv)` method that encapsulates config dir
lookup, path encoding, and candidate directory matching. Both
`listSessions` and `readSessionTranscript` now delegate to this single
method.
- **`src/main/orchestrators/claude-code-provider.test.ts`**: Added
`resolveProjectDir` test suite covering:
  - Returns empty/null when projects dir doesn't exist
  - Resolves with leading dash
  - Falls back to without leading dash
  - Honors `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` from profileEnv
  - Returns empty/null when no matching project dir exists
  - Both methods resolve the same directory for the same cwd

## Test Plan
- [x] All 57 tests in `claude-code-provider.test.ts` pass
- [x] 7 new tests specifically exercise the shared resolution logic
- [x] No lint errors in changed files
- [x] TypeScript compiles (pre-existing unrelated type error in
`file-watch-service.ts`)

## Manual Validation
No manual validation needed — this is a pure refactor with no behavior
change. The extracted method is private and tested through the existing
public API.

---------

The build/images/justfile was hardcoded to use podman, but GitHub

commit.message:
Fix race condition in spawnAgent map tracking vs async spawn (#724)

## Summary
- Fix race condition where failed async spawn operations leave orphaned
entries in agent tracking maps (`agentProjectMap`,
`agentOrchestratorMap`, `headlessAgentSet`, `structuredAgentSet`)
- Wrap spawn operations in try/catch that calls `untrackAgent` on
failure, ensuring clean state after errors

Fixes #529

## Changes
- **`agent-system.ts`**: Wrapped the post-map-assignment spawn logic in
an outer try/catch that calls `untrackAgent(params.agentId)` on any
error, then re-throws. Added inner try/catch blocks for structured and
headless paths to also clean up their mode-specific sets before the
error propagates.
- **`agent-system.test.ts`**: Added 5 new test cases covering:
  - PTY spawn failure cleans up tracking maps
- Structured spawn failure cleans up tracking maps and
`structuredAgentSet`
- Headless spawn failure cleans up tracking maps and `headlessAgentSet`
  - Original error is propagated after cleanup

## Test Plan
- [x] PTY spawn failure (`buildSpawnCommand` rejects) → all tracking
maps cleaned up
- [x] Structured spawn failure (`startStructuredSession` rejects) → maps
+ `structuredAgentSet` cleaned up
- [x] Headless spawn failure (`spawnHeadless` throws) → maps +
`headlessAgentSet` cleaned up
- [x] Original error is re-thrown to caller after cleanup
- [x] All 73 existing + new tests pass
- [x] No regressions in happy-path spawn tracking behavior

## Manual Validation
No manual validation needed — the fix is purely defensive error handling
tested via unit tests.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix preload bridge sync test excluded from test runner (#723)

## Summary
- Add `integration` project scope to `vitest.config.ts` so
`test/**/*.test.ts` files are included in the test runner
- Sync `test/setup-renderer.ts` mock with 22 missing methods from
`src/preload/index.ts` that the now-running sync test detected

## Changes
- **vitest.config.ts**: Added a fourth project scope `integration` with
`include: ['test/**/*.test.ts']` and `environment: 'node'`, so the
preload bridge sync test (`test/preload-bridge-sync.test.ts`) is no
longer excluded
- **test/setup-renderer.ts**: Added 22 missing mock methods across 6
namespaces:
- `file`: `watchStart`, `watchStop`, `onWatchEvent`, `offWatchEvent`,
`search`
- `plugin`: `listProjectInjections`, `cleanupProjectInjections`,
`listOrphanedPluginIds`, `registerManifest`
- `marketplace`: `listCustomMarketplaces`, `addCustomMarketplace`,
`removeCustomMarketplace`, `toggleCustomMarketplace` (also added missing
`incompatibleUpdates` field to `getPluginUpdatesStatus`)
- `app`: `getSoundSettings`, `saveSoundSettings`, `listSoundPacks`,
`importSoundPack`, `deleteSoundPack`, `getSoundData`
  - `annex`: `onAgentSpawned`
  - `window`: `broadcastAgentState`, `onAgentStateChanged`

## Test Plan
- [x] `npx vitest run --project integration` — sync test passes (2/2
tests green)
- [x] `npm test` — full suite passes (5714 tests, 219/222 files pass; 3
pre-existing failures unrelated to this change)
- [x] `npm run lint` — no new lint errors (2 pre-existing errors in
unrelated files)
- [x] `npm run typecheck` — no new type errors (1 pre-existing picomatch
error)

## Manual Validation
No manual steps needed — this is a test infrastructure fix. The sync
test will now automatically catch future preload-mock drift.

Fixes #617

commit.message:
Add realistic built-in plugin tests to plugin-loader (#631) (#722)

## Summary
- Fixes #631: Plugin loader tests previously mocked `getBuiltinPlugins`
with empty arrays, bypassing the primary function of the plugin loader
- Adds test cases using realistic built-in plugin data (hub, terminal,
files) to verify the discovery and loading pipeline end-to-end
- Adds missing test coverage for `getBuiltinProjectPluginIds()`

## Changes
- **Realistic built-in plugin discovery tests** (new `describe` block
inside `initializePluginSystem()`):
- Registers all three built-in plugins (hub=dual, terminal=project,
files=project) with correct source and scope
  - Sets modules for all built-in plugins in the store
  - Auto-enables all three at app level
  - Calls `registerManifest` for each built-in plugin
- Verifies project-scoped builtins are not activated during init (only
on project switch)
  - Verifies direct activation works correctly for each scope type
  - Merges persisted app-enabled config with auto-enabled builtins
- Activates project-scoped builtins correctly via `handleProjectSwitch`
  - Writes startup marker containing all enabled builtins

- **`getBuiltinProjectPluginIds()` tests** (new `describe` block):
  - Returns project-scoped and dual-scoped default-enabled IDs
  - Excludes app-scoped built-in plugins
  - Excludes non-default built-in plugins
  - Returns empty array when no builtins match

## Test Plan
- [x] All 98 plugin-loader tests pass (was 86, added 12 new)
- [x] Full test suite passes (5726 tests, 3 pre-existing failures
unrelated to changes)
- [x] TypeCheck passes (1 pre-existing picomatch type error)
- [x] Lint passes (2 pre-existing errors unrelated to changes)

## Manual Validation
No manual validation needed — this is a test-only change.

commit.message:
fix: skip constants with secret-like names (#7)

* fix: skip constants with secret-like names in parser

Defense-in-depth: extractConstants() now skips constants whose names
end with KEY, SECRET, TOKEN, PASSWORD, or CREDENTIAL (as underscore-
delimited word segments). Prevents accidental credential storage in
the knowledge graph.

Non-secret constants with partial matches (KEYBOARD_SHORTCUT,
TOKEN_LIMIT, SECRET_SAUCE_RECIPE) are correctly preserved — the
pattern only matches suffix positions.

docs: Add skill update workflow and documentation

Added documentation and tooling for keeping global skills in sync with repository:

## Files Added
- ultrapilot/ultrapilot/scripts/update-skills.mjs
  - Syncs skill files from repo to ~/.claude/skills/
  - Run after pulling changes from GitHub
  - Makes updating easy and automatic

## Files Modified
- README.md
  - Added "Updating After Pulling Changes" section
  - Documents update-skills.mjs workflow
  - Clear instructions for keeping skills up to date

## Workflow
1. User clones repo and runs install.mjs
2. User pulls changes from GitHub
3. User runs update-skills.mjs to sync skills
4. Global skills directory updated with latest changes

This makes it easy to keep the UltraPilot skill up to date across multiple machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace fragile structural tests with AST-based checks (#721)

## Summary
- Replace regex/text-parsing structural tests with TypeScript AST-based
analysis and runtime imports, making them resilient to formatting
changes
- Fixes #658 — structural tests were fragile to formatter config changes
and could produce false failures/passes

## Changes

### `src/renderer/App.structural.test.ts`
- **Replaced `getJsxReturnBlocks()`** (indentation-dependent regex) with
AST-based `findReturnPaths()` that walks the TypeScript AST to find
return statements and their containing if-conditions
- **Replaced regex-based selector counting** (`/use\w+Store\(\(s\)/g`)
with `findStoreSelectors()` using AST call expression analysis
- **Replaced regex-based useEffect counting** with `countCallsInBody()`
using AST
- **Replaced complex regex patterns** (try/catch wrapping, project
switch useEffect) with precise AST-based checks (`hasTryCatchAround()`,
`findUseEffectWithDep()`)
- **Replaced import verification** from string `contains` to AST import
declaration analysis (`findImportedNames()`, `findImportModules()`)
- **Replaced React hooks detection** in event bridge from regex
word-boundary matching to AST import checking
- **Kept simple string-contains checks** where already
formatting-resilient (identifier names like `'clearStaleStatuses'`,
import paths)
- Added comprehensive documentation explaining why structural tests
exist and the AST approach

### `src/shared/ipc-channel-sync.test.ts`
- **Replaced `extractChannelMap()`** (line-by-line regex parsing of
ipc-channels.ts source text) with direct runtime import of the `IPC`
object and programmatic walking via `buildChannelMap()`
- Removed unused `_extractChannelEntries()` function
- Added documentation about the runtime import approach
- Kept simple identifier-pattern matching for phantom reference checks
(already formatting-resilient)

## Test Plan
- [x] All 481 structural tests pass (41 in App.structural.test.ts, 440
in ipc-channel-sync.test.ts)
- [x] Full test suite passes (5712 tests, 3 pre-existing failures
unrelated to changes)
- [x] TypeScript type check passes (only pre-existing picomatch issue)
- [x] Tests verify the same structural properties as before but via AST
nodes instead of regex
- [x] Tests are now resilient to whitespace, indentation, and line break
changes

## Manual Validation
Run a formatter (e.g. `prettier`) on App.tsx or ipc-channels.ts and
verify the structural tests still pass — this was the core fragility
that this PR eliminates.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Process audit tools one at a time: help → assemble → confirm → run

Each tool is handled independently:
- whereis to locate it
- --help fetched from that binary only
- single LLM call with just that tool's help to assemble one command
- confirm and run before moving to the next tool

Eliminates bulk help dump to LLM; smaller focused prompts per tool.
Added 'q' option to quit mid-audit.

siblings, with dimmed color for disabled directions.

commit.message:
Convert getWorktreeStatus to async with parallel git calls (#720)

## Summary
- Convert `getWorktreeStatus` from blocking `execSync` calls to async
`exec` via `execGitAsync`, running git status, base branch detection,
and remote check in parallel with `Promise.all`
- Convert `detectBaseBranch` and `deleteSaveAsPatch` to async (shared
dependency)
- Fix minor parsing bug: use `trimEnd()` instead of `trim()` to preserve
leading status chars in `git status --porcelain` output

Fixes #644

## Changes
- `detectBaseBranch`: converted from sync `execSync` to async
`execGitAsync`
- `getWorktreeStatus`: converted to `async`, runs 3 independent git
commands (`git status --porcelain`, `git rev-parse --verify`, `git
remote`) in parallel via `Promise.all`; `git log` runs after base branch
is resolved
- `deleteSaveAsPatch`: updated signature to `async` since it calls
`detectBaseBranch`
- Status line parsing: changed `.trim()` to `.trimEnd()` to avoid
stripping leading space from status indicators like ` M`
- Tests: updated existing tests to use `async/await`, added happy-path
test verifying parallel execution and correct parsing, added
error-handling test

## Test Plan
- [x] Existing `getWorktreeStatus` tests updated to async and passing
- [x] New test: valid worktree returns parsed status with uncommitted
files, unpushed commits, and remote detection
- [x] New test: all git commands failing returns graceful defaults
(empty arrays, hasRemote=false)
- [x] All 76 agent-config tests pass
- [x] All 35 agent-handlers tests pass
- [x] Full test suite passes (5714 tests, 3 pre-existing renderer
failures unrelated)
- [x] Typecheck passes (1 pre-existing picomatch error unrelated)
- [x] Lint passes (2 pre-existing errors unrelated)

## Manual Validation
- Open Clubhouse with a project that has multiple durable agents
- Open the delete agent dialog (which calls `getWorktreeStatus`)
- Verify worktree status loads without blocking the UI
- Verify uncommitted files, unpushed commits, and remote status display
correctly

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Migrate DeviceRepository to Result<D, E> API (#919)

Change all 9 DeviceRepository suspend functions from throwing to
returning Result<Unit, DataError>. Try-catch is now confined to
DefaultDeviceRepository (Room/SQLite boundary). All UseCases
propagate Result, ViewModels use when-on-Result, and CrashProof
tests verify error handling via Result.Error.

Restructure Build page: hero reframe + 3-row dashboard

Hero: "Building a company with agents" — positions as live operating
dashboard, not just a build log.

- Operations: daily agent cost ($2-5/day), skills running, days operating

commit.message:
Remove CDN cache on /api/leads — was serving stale 88-lead response

s-maxage=60 caused Vercel CDN to cache the leads response, so clicking
Refresh returned old cached data instead of the 1007 leads in KV.
Changed to no-store so every request goes through to the KV cache layer.

Removed the duplicates; page now uses the shared constants.

feat: Implement full template library with Fabric.js v7 editor

Add comprehensive image template management system with interactive canvas editor.

Backend:
- Add image_template_library table to store templates with JSON data
- Implement 6 API routes for library CRUD and agent assignments:
  - GET /api/image-templates/library (fetch all templates)
  - POST /api/image-templates/library (create new template)
  - PUT /api/image-templates/library/:id (update template)
  - DELETE /api/image-templates/library/:id (delete template)
  - GET /api/image-templates/:agentId/assignments (get agent template assignments)
  - PUT /api/image-templates/:agentId/assignments (update agent assignments)
- Fix IPv4/IPv6 binding issue: explicitly bind to 127.0.0.1 for Vite proxy compatibility

Frontend Components:
- Create TemplateEditor.tsx: Main editor component with Fabric.js canvas
- Create TemplateCanvas.tsx: Fabric.js v7 canvas implementation with element manipulation
- Create PropertiesPanel.tsx: Dynamic property editor for text, image, shape, gradient, QR code elements
- Create LayerPanel.tsx: Element layer management with visibility and z-index controls
- Create ElementPanel.tsx: Quick-add buttons for element types
- Create templateTypes.ts: TypeScript definitions and factory functions for templates
- Create TemplatesTab.tsx: Template library management UI with CRUD operations
- Rewrite AgentImageTemplateTab.tsx: Simplified template selector (620 lines → 150 lines)

Navigation:
- Restructure admin sidebar into collapsible groups:
  - Content (Agents, Workflows, Templates)
  - Configuration (Content Options, API Keys, Pricing)
  - Integrations (Notifications, Webhooks, Brand Monitor)
  - Team (Members)
- Add Templates item to settings navigation
- Support independent expansion state per group with localStorage persistence

Styling:
- All new components use Tailwind CSS with Emerald/Indigo color scheme
- Replace all shadcn components with plain HTML elements for consistency
- Implement SVG thumbnail rendering for template previews

Bug Fixes:
- Fix Vite proxy configuration: use correct backend port (3000)
- Remove unused constant warnings
- Remove unnecessary React imports from editor components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Windows compatibility for OpenCode config paths and execution

- Add OpenCodeNativeConfigPath() using os.UserConfigDir() to resolve
  correct config path per OS (Windows: %APPDATA%, Linux: ~/.config)
- Add Windows binary search paths (npm, bun, scoop) in GetOpenCodeBinary()
- Pass OpenCode prompt via stdin instead of command-line argument to
  avoid cmd.exe escaping issues with .cmd shims on Windows
- Use OpenCodeNativeConfigPath() in MCP registration handlers

commit.message:
feat: add VRP withdrawal harvester + dataset

harvest_vrp.py extracts BLUE/YELLOW withdrawal events from Project Agora
VRP sessions — the ground-truth WITNESS signal.

20 events harvested (18 BLUE, 2 YELLOW):
- 8 BLUE events with full chain-of-thought reasoning preserved
- 9 with CoT blocks showing threshold articulation structure
- Quality: sparse on clean text, rich in CoT reasoning

These replace the low-entropy IRIS proxy used in v0.1.
VRP CoT blocks are the phenomenological threshold descriptions.
v0.2 will train on these as WITNESS ground truth.

Show actual asymmetric Templeton variance data, not a rounded ± approximation (#31)

- Remove ±N" simplification; show the real +N" / -M" from the data
- Use fmtAimVariance for unit-aware formatting (imperial keeps raw precision,
  metric converts to cm) — no fmtInches rounding that was losing data
- Use − HTML entity for the minus sign to fix rendering across browsers
- Rows with both sides show "+1.2\" / −1.5\""; minus-only rows show "−1.5\""
